title: Add query.EpicsNeedingPlanning predicate to internal/query
description:
Foundation tick for phase 2 (spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 'tk changes' + §3). Both tk next and tk graph will consume this.

New files: internal/query/plan.go + internal/query/plan_test.go (follow the style of internal/query/ready.go and ready_test.go).

Function: EpicsNeedingPlanning(candidates []tick.Tick, allTicks ...[]tick.Tick) []tick.Tick
An epic 'needs planning' when ALL of:
- Type == tick.TypeEpic and Status is open (NOT in_progress — an in_progress epic is being worked)
- Not blocked: every BlockedBy entry is closed or missing (same blocker semantics as isReadyWithOptions in ready.go: missing blocker = closed)
- Not deferred (DeferUntil nil or past) and not awaiting human (IsAwaitingHuman() false) — same exclusions as ready.go
- Has ZERO children in allTicks (no tick, any status, has Parent == epic.ID). NOTE: an epic whose children are all closed does NOT need planning — it needs closing; zero-children is the test, not zero-open-children.
Use the allTicks variadic lookup pattern from ready.go (candidates may be a filtered subset; children/blockers may live outside it).

Test cases (table-driven like ready_test.go):
- open unblocked childless epic -> returned
- epic with one open child -> not returned
- epic with only closed children -> not returned
- epic blocked by an open epic -> not returned; blocked by a closed/missing epic -> returned
- in_progress epic, awaiting epic, deferred epic (future DeferUntil) -> not returned
- non-epic tick -> not returned

Run: go test ./internal/query/...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./internal/query/... passes with the new table-driven tests; go vet ./... clean
